author: jsign
content:
Could finally read the article, very cool!

Some questions for <@745384946310578317> / <@976542403269910539> (numerating since might be easier to reply):
1. Is the process of building the final ELF that you used for benchmarks part of a transparent CI that we can take a look at?
2. Would be possible through that CI (if exists) you can put this ELF in releases in the `zilkworkm` repo? We would like to pull all official ELFs from team’s repos (as source of truth) and probably re-publish them in `ere-guests` for convenience. This would make `zkevm-benchmark-workload` not have to rely in custom URLs for downloading elfs.
3.  Do you have (or have plans for) supporting Zisk v0.18?
4. Do you have plans to accept also as untouched guest inputs the current EEST fixtures `statelessInputBytes`? We are trying to get everyone accepting this to have a 100% pure dataset run style to measure the performance for everyone — from that point forward we can learn lessons that you have experimented on how maybe change the specs in a way that can allow more efficient validation of witness or similar; but having exceptions on some guest programs having custom inputs is a bit complicated. Is at least having a separate variant of Zilkworm accepting current EEST `statelessInputBytes` as is possible? (You can still keep optimizing your other custom variant and hopefully at some point we can unify them)

cc <@944007601400741989> <@466102668138512394> for visibility
